text: If you're a trader, you probably watched

their own version of it. But have you
ever wondered whether any of these

that one thing got me a setup that
returned 531%

So, what is the 9:30 a.m. strategy? Even
if you already know it, stick with me.
I've seen a lot of people think they
know it, but actually don't. At 9:30
a.m. Eastern Standard Time, you mark the
high and low of a specific candle. For
example, a 15-minute candle. That high
and low range is called the opening
range.
Then you watch to see if candle breaks
out and closes out of that range using
the same time frame or a lower one.
The basic assumption is that whichever
direction price breaks out, it will
continue in that direction.
So, you enter in the direction of the
breakout, in this case, long position.
Place a limit order at the edge of the
range. Stop loss goes on the opposite
side of the range, and take profit is
set at a predefined risk-reward ratio,
like 1:2 or 1:3.
Now, this is the most basic version. But
here's where it gets interesting. Every
guru claims this basic version doesn't
work. And that you need their specific
secret to make it profitable.
Some say you need to wait for a retest
before entering. Others say wait for a
fair value gap during the breakout, or
use candlestick patterns, and etc.
And not only that, they all say
different things about which time frame
to use, where to put your stop loss,
what risk-reward ratio to go with, and
etc.
There's even a guy named Casper who

what we're going to find out. So, the

He says this is what separates winners

For the rest of the rules, he stick to

He also claims this works on every

So, is that true?

That means you take the high and low of

Fourth variable is the risk to reward

So, here's the lesson. All those gurus

If you've ever back-tested a single

There's no such thing as one strategy
that works on everything because every
asset moves differently. Some trend
hard, some barely trend at all, some are

price action is just too different.
That's why you have to find the best

There were three key findings that

First, the basic 9:30 a.m. strategy

Euro and gold. No retest, no fair value

Second, the risk-reward ratio. For both

Third, the time frame for defining the
